mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% indicates near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a rising 3-month moving average threshold, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp above its trailing 3-month average
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE trigger fires, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong retail sales (RSAFS rising) and weakening housing starts (HOUST falling) signals an unsustainable consumption-led expansion masking underlying demand fragility, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone carries this signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth positive while HOUST 3-month growth negative
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 457727 and HOUST at 1189 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-up/HOUST-down condition fails to produce negative mean forward returns distinguishable from the single-series baselines, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC (delta IC < 0.02) versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, accelerating INDPRO growth reflects capacity strain and margin pressure rather than genuine demand strength, producing a short-horizon negative reaction in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in overheating risk; in early-cycle recovery the same signal is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.13 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Sign of the INDPRO-return relationship does not flip between early-cycle and late-cycle subsamples, or Rank IC magnitude in late-cycle regime is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% following a period near zero, tightening financial conditions raise borrowing costs and compress valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% from a prior sub-1.0% base
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS currently at 0.39%, near lower bound
failure_condition: In historical episodes where FEDFUNDS crosses the 2.0% threshold from a low base, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth combined with a rising UNRATE indicates a genuine labor market turning point (not noise), jointly predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; each series alone is too noisy to carry the signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth below 0.1% while UNRATE rises MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144448 thousand and UNRATE at 4.9% in latest snapshot
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration/UNRATE-rise condition does not produce negative forward returns distinguishable from either single-series signal, or combined Rank IC improvement over best single proxy is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects durable aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) as an always-on trend effect.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16575.1 billion chained dollars as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 QoQ growth and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns following positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, an acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth signals rising odds of central bank tightening and squeezed real consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; in a low-inflation regime the same acceleration has no reliable directional effect.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.927 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The CPI-return relationship shows no significant sign difference between high-inflation and low-inflation subsamples, or Rank IC in high-inflation regime is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
